title: polemonium carneum subsp. luteum
intro: Polemonium carneum subsp. luteum, also known as the yellow Jacob's ladder, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Polemonium carneum plant, belonging to the Polemoniaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Polemonium carneum subsp. luteum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs, and provide relief from colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its availability and usage may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
